macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Job Growth Slows Sharply in July, Unemployment Holds at 3.9%
items.summary: The U.S. economy added 157,000 jobs in July, falling below analyst expectations, while the unemployment rate remained steady at 3.9%, signaling a cooling labor market amid persistent inflation concerns.

items.title: Fed Under New Leadership: Kevin Warsh Takes Over as Jerome Powell Concludes Term
items.summary: Former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh has assumed the role of Federal Reserve Chair following Jerome Powell’s eight-year term, with the new leadership signaling a less accommodative monetary policy and a near-term rate cut now appearing highly unlikely.

items.title: Inflation Reaches 4.2% in May, Highest in Three Years, as Energy Costs Surge 11%
items.summary: Consumer price inflation rose to 4.2% year-over-year in May, driven by an 11% increase in energy costs, while producer prices climbed 6.4%, reinforcing expectations of a potential rate hike before year-end.

items.title: S&P 500 Posts 15% Gain in Q2 Despite Early-Year Drop, Emerging Markets Outperform
items.summary: U.S. equities rallied strongly in the second quarter with the S&P 500 finishing up 15%, while emerging market equities surged 24% and developed international stocks rose nearly 11%, amid easing geopolitical tensions and a ceasefire in Iran.

items.title: Gold Slides 14% in Q2, Commodities Retreat 8% After U.S.-Iran Memorandum
items.summary: Gold took a breather in the second quarter, sliding 14%, while commodities rose initially but retreated 8% after the U.S. and Iran reached a memorandum of understanding, reflecting shifting market dynamics amid geopolitical developments.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopens, Trade Policy Uncertainty Declines, Boosting Business Planning
items.summary: The reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and declining trade policy uncertainty are viewed as bullish for business planning and hiring, though rising freight rates and mild supply chain distress suggest ongoing economic pressures.

items.title: UN Raises Global Inflation Forecast to 3.9% for 2026 Amid New Energy Shock
items.summary: The United Nations has raised its global inflation forecast for 2026 to 3.9% due to a new energy shock linked to Middle East conflict, signaling that crises are becoming a permanent feature of economic life rather than temporary disruptions.

items.title: Market Narrative Shifts to Rate Hike Expectations as Fed Turns Hawkish
items.summary: Driven by strong April CPI, hot May non-farm payrolls, and a hawkish Fed stance, the market narrative now suggests the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz could overheat the economy, forcing the Fed to raise interest rates soon.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Breaks $61,000 Resistance as Ethereum Surges 4.2%
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near $61,292, up 0.80% over 24 hours, confirming a breakout above crucial resistance, while Ethereum outperformed with a 4.22% daily gain to $1,698, extending its weekly gain past 10%.

items.title: BlackRock Transfers 20,000 BTC to Coinbase Amid Market Cap Rise
items.summary: BlackRock moved over 20,000 BTC to Coinbase across four consecutive days as the total crypto market cap rose to $2.13 trillion, with Solana and XXP also posting notable weekly gains.

items.title: Ondo Finance Launches SEC-Compliant Tokenized Products Tied to BlackRock ETF
items.summary: Ondo Finance introduced SEC-compliant tokenized products linked to BlackRock’s IVV ETF and Micron shares, utilizing a third-party custody framework issued by the SEC earlier this year.

items.title: SEC Acknowledges Past Crypto ETF Mishandling, Promises Orderly Review Process
items.summary: SEC Division of Investment Management Director Brian Daly admitted the agency mishandled past crypto ETF approvals and stated a more orderly review process is being developed for roughly 200 monthly applications.

items.title: Russia Confirms Digital Ruble Launch Date of September 1, Cautious on Stablecoins
items.summary: Russia’s central bank confirmed the digital ruble will launch on September 1, while maintaining a cautious stance on stablecoins, framing them only as a supplement to CBDC-based settlement.

items.title: Trump Defends $1.4B Crypto Income as Ethics Critics Raise Concerns
items.summary: President Trump defended his family’s $1.4 billion crypto-related profits from 2025, calling conflict-of-interest reports a non-issue, while critics and his niece allege a history of corruption tied to meme token sales.

items.title: JPMorgan Warns of 'Avoidable Two-Way Risk' from Strategy’s New Bitcoin Sale Policy
items.summary: JPMorgan warned that Strategy’s new Bitcoin sale policy introduces avoidable two-way risk to the market, given the company holds nearly 4% of total BTC supply.

items.title: Crypto Donor Anonymously Posts Bail for Empire State Building Climbers
items.summary: A Rainbet crypto donor anonymously posted the bail for the Empire State Building climbers within 24 hours, sparking widespread social media attention.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Flipkart Builds In-House LLM as AI Writes 40% of Code
items.summary: Flipkart has deployed over 250 AI models and is now building specialized e-commerce large language models, with AI writing 35–40% of its software code.

items.title: Machine Learning Finds Bitcoin Trading Signals but Not Proven Edge
items.summary: A test of 1,856 ML trading strategies on Bitcoin and S&P 500 data revealed interesting signals, but none were strong enough to be considered a proven tradable edge after full validation.

items.title: Apple Unveils New Siri AI Updates at WWDC 2026
items.summary: Apple announced significant Siri AI enhancements at WWDC 2026, including improved natural language processing and contextual awareness, in Tim Cook’s final WWDC appearance.

items.title: AI Assistant Rewrites Its Own Code via Recursive Learning
items.summary: A new AI assistant demonstrates self-improvement capabilities by rewriting its own code using recursive learning, raising questions about the feasibility of autonomous AI advancement.

items.title: GitHub Highlights Deep Learning and AI Research Trends
items.summary: GitHub, the Microsoft-owned code platform, is sharing breaking news and insights on deep learning, AI, ML, and math, reflecting growing industry focus on these fields.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: State of Decay 3 Faces Cancellation Risk Amid Microsoft Studio Shakeups
items.summary: Reports indicate State of Decay 3 may be canceled as Microsoft explores selling Undead Labs, with multiple studios including Double Fine and Ninja Theory reportedly on the chopping block.

items.title: Sony Reveals New PlayStation Controller and Announces Nioh 3 at State of Play
items.summary: During Sony's June State of Play, the company unveiled a new PlayStation controller with an unexpected design and announced Team Ninja's Nioh 3, a new soulslike entry.

items.title: Arc System Works Unveils Marvel Tokon Fighting Game with Open Beta in July
items.summary: Arc System Works and PlayStation announced Marvel Tokon, a new fighting game featuring Marvel characters and tag battles, with an open beta scheduled from July 24–26 on PS5 and PC.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Announces New Force of Law Warbond for Cooperative Shooter
items.summary: Helldivers 2 surprised fans by revealing the upcoming Force of Law Warbond, adding new content to the popular cooperative shooter.

items.title: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture Reveal New Over-the-Top Action Game
items.summary: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture announced a new action game described as a combination of No More Heroes and Dead Rising, continuing their signature over-the-top style.

items.title: XDefiant Executive Producer Steps Away from Industry After Game's One-Year Run
items.summary: As fans say goodbye to XDefiant after just one year, the game's executive producer announced he will be stepping away from the video game industry permanently.

items.title: Pokemon GO Brings Back Community Day Voting for August 2025 Edition
items.summary: Pokemon GO has reintroduced Community Day voting for its upcoming August 2025 edition, with four Pokemon as candidates for the spotlight.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Tesla Model Y Long Wheelbase Launches in US with Six Seats
items.summary: Tesla has started taking orders for the Model Y Long Wheelbase in the US and Puerto Rico, featuring six seats and a $62,000 price tag.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold Debuts as Most Advanced Foldable Yet
items.summary: Samsung unveiled its Galaxy Z TriFold, a redesigned dual-hinge foldable with upgraded materials and a 10-inch display that unfolds from a pocketable frame.

items.title: Form Energy Secures $405M for 100-Hour Battery Development
items.summary: Form Energy, a company producing a longer-lasting alternative to lithium batteries, announced $405 million in funding to accelerate development of its 100-hour battery.

items.title: X Returns to Brazil After Over a Month of Suspension
items.summary: Elon Musk's social media platform X is back in Brazil after complying with all judicial demands following its suspension due to clashes with a justice on the country's highest court.

items.title: Spotify Launches Free Streaming Option in South Korea
items.summary: Spotify has introduced a free streaming tier in South Korea, expanding access to its music platform in the region.

items.title: Space Startups See Funding Growth as Investors Bet on Capex Ventures
items.summary: A report indicates rising investor interest in space startups focused on capex-intensive ventures, signaling a funding growth trend in the sector.

items.title: US Considers Breaking Up Google to Eliminate Search Monopoly
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice is weighing remedies in its antitrust case against Google, including potentially forcing the company to sell parts of its business.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Quantum Computer Using Standard Silicon
items.summary: Quantum Motion has built the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication, a milestone that could accelerate scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Jeff Clarke Takes Over Dell's PC Business to Reverse Years of Drift
items.summary: Jeff Clarke is assuming leadership of Dell's PC division as the company aims to restore its XPS halo and compete in a market where fundamentals still win.

items.title: American Water Cyberattack Highlights Critical Infrastructure Risks
items.summary: A continuing cyberattack on the nation's largest regulated water utility has renewed focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure.
